title: Bangladesh video falsely viral as Kerala man attacked by daughters over sexual abuse
body_text:
A three-minute video that appears to be CCTV footage of a bleeding man has gone viral. In the footage, two men can be seen opening a collapsible gate, after which another man walks out, howling as he bleeds and eventually walks away. Social media users have claimed that the man from Kerala seen in the video raped his own daughters, and the daughters retaliated and severed his genitals.
An X user, Boiled Anda (@AmitLeliSlayer) shared the video on June 30 with the same claim. (Archive)
This post received 6.2 million views. It is to be noted that this user has been found sharing communal propaganda and disinformation several times in the past.
Another X user, Harman Singh Kapoor, shared the same video, making similar allegations and asserting that the incident took place in Kerala, India. (Archive)
This post garnered nearly 100,000 voews.
Several other users have shared the same video across various social media platforms with similar claims. A few of these posts are shown in the gallery below:
Fact Check
A reverse image search of a few keyframes from the video led us to the Facebook page of News21, whose logo appears in the footage. News21, a Bangladesh-based news outlet, published the video on June 29, 2026.
The Bangla caption can be translated as “In Bhanga, Faridpur, a wife allegedly cut off her husband’s genitalia while he was asleep. Later, local residents called the emergency number 999, and the woman was handed over to the police. | NEWS 21”
We also found several video reports on the incident by Bangladeshi media outlets, including ADP Bangla, Cumilla News, and SuryaUday Khulna.
Following a keyword search, we found reports confirming that the incident took place in Hogladangi Sadardi village in Bhanga Upazila of Faridpur district in Bangladesh.
The victim, Hanif Sheikh, had his genitals allegedly severed by his second wife, Sumaya Aktar Sumi, following an argument the night before. This act reportedly occurred while Hanif was asleep on June 29. According to one report, Hanif had relationships with multiple women and that disputes over these alleged affairs frequently caused tension in the family.
According to Aajker Patrik, the officer-in-charge of Bhanga police station, Mohammad Mizanur Rahman, stated, “We arrested the woman at the scene after receiving information via the emergency number 999. No written complaint has been received regarding this incident so far, but legal action will be taken if a written complaint is submitted.”
To sum up, the video depicts an incident from Bangladesh, which has no connection to Kerala or India. In the video, the man’s genitals were severed by his wife. The claims circulating online are false and misleading.

title: Viral video does not show Pune Fort case accused Siya Goyal dancing
body_text:
A 36-second-long video of two women dancing has gone viral on social media with users claiming that it features Siya Goyal, one of the accused in the alleged murder of 26-year-old Ketan Agarwal in Pune’s Lohagad Fort.
On June 23, Pune rural police arrested Siya Goyal (20) and Chetan Chaudhary (22) for allegedly murdering Goyal’s fiancé, Ketan Agarwal. They have been booked under Sections 103 (murder) and 61 (criminal conspiracy) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS).
As per reports, Goyal and Chaudhary had been involved in a romantic relationship and allegedly conspired to murder Agarwal. Goyal and Agarwal went on a trek to Pune’s Lohagad Fort on June 18, where Agarwal fell nearly 400 feet into a gorge. Investigation later revealed it was not an accidental fall.
It is against this backdrop that the said video has gone viral.
X page TeluguBulletin.com (@TeluguBulletin) shared the afore mentioned clip on June 27, claiming that the video featured Goyal. The post garnered over 10,000 views. (Archive)
Siya Goyal’s statements about Ketan have sparked fresh debate online. After she claimed she was unhappy with certain aspects of their relationship, many social media users questioned how the couple had appeared so comfortable during their pre-wedding shoot. The discussion… pic.twitter.com/Qflf1xgyvH
— TeluguBulletin.com (@TeluguBulletin) June 27, 2026
Another X user, @thecaspiansk, posted the video with a caption saying: “Yes she was not happy with wedding”. This post has received more than 163,000 views. (Archive)
Yes she was not happy with wedding pic.twitter.com/olMFKTQo9O
— CASPIAN (@thecaspiansk) June 27, 2026
Several other users shared the same clip and amplified the claim that the video showed Goyal dancing. Below are a few instances:
Fact Check
To verify this claim, we broke down the video into several keyframes and ran a reverse image search on some of them. This led us to an Instagram post shared on June 21, 2026. This post carried the same video and had been shared by user @sanikaajagtap. The user had tagged another account with the username @anushkawanii.
View this post on Instagram
Social media users have misidentified Anushka Wani (@anushkawanii) as Siya Goyal, as the two women share some facial similarities. However, upon taking a look at Wani’s profile, it is clear that the video features her and not Goyal. Below is a comparison of their faces:
Therefore, the claim associated with the viral clip that it features murder accused Siya Goyal is false.

title: Viral ‘pen bomb’ warning messages baseless; police refute rumour
body_text:
A photo of what appears to be five grenade-shaped pens is being widely shared on social media. Along with it, an audio message and photographs of injured people are rapidly circulating on WhatsApp. The message warns people against bombs resembling pen-like objects scattered in various places.
“Anyone who presses the button on top of this pen will cause it to explode, causing injuries to the face, hands, or body.” The message also urges recipients to forward it to every group and inform both children and adults.
Alt News received multiple requests on its WhatsApp helpline (+91 7600011160) seeking verification of the claim and the truth behind the alleged “pen bomb.”
In an article published by AIMA Media and News First Prime, images of the purported pen bomb and injured individuals were used to claim that pen-shaped miniature bombs had been found in different locations in Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan. According to these reports, people picked up the objects and pressed their buttons, causing accidents. These incidents caused fear and anxiety among locals.
Furthermore, referring to the viral images, the articles stated, “All citizens have been urged not to pick up objects resembling pens in order to avoid accidents. The message also appeals to people to share this warning as widely as possible so that they and their families can remain safe.”
Meanwhile, Zee News’ official Facebook, Instagram and YouTube accounts, as well as State Mirror Hindi and Vikalp Times Media, published articles displaying images of the grenade-shaped pen and claiming that “Bulandshahr Police has issued an important advisory for the general public. Citizens have been urged to stay away from suspicious objects resembling pens, as these could be explosives or bombs. The police have warned that if such a pen-shaped suspicious device or object is spotted on roadsides, in fields, or in isolated places, people should not touch or move it under any circumstances. Instead, they should immediately inform the nearest police station or contact the helpline.”
Several users have also shared these images, claiming that police in Delhi, Chhattisgarh and other parts of the country had issued alerts regarding pen bombs. Some even described the entire episode as a Pakistani conspiracy.
Fact Check
Alt News investigated the claims regarding the alleged police advisory.
We found that the official X account of Bulandshahr Police had replied to a post by Vikalp Times Media and rejected the claim. The police clarified that neither the district magistrate nor the senior superintendent of police in Bulandshahr had issued any advisory or warning regarding suspicious pen-shaped objects. The administration described the viral claim as misleading.
#News “सावधान! यह पेन नहीं, मौत का सामान भी हो सकता है : पुलिस की एडवाइजरी”https://t.co/CFrtMHPIMl#Bulandshahar #Police #Pen #Viral #Trending #SawaiMadhopurApp #VikalpTimes @bulandshahrpol pic.twitter.com/oqfL9W1pFt
— Vikalp Times (@VikalpTimes) June 13, 2026
During the investigation, we also found a video posted by the official account of the Raipur Police Commissionerate in Chhattisgarh, featuring ACP Ramakant Sahu of Civil Lines. He appealed to the public not to believe or circulate rumours.
Sahu stated that no such incident had occurred in Raipur, Chhattisgarh, or anywhere else in the country. According to him, the message appeared to have been spread solely to create panic. He urged people not to pay attention to such rumours and to inform the police if they had any concerns.
अफवाहों से सावधान 🚨
सोशल मीडिया पर प्रसारित ‘विस्फोटक पेन’ संबंधी खबर एवं तस्वीरें भ्रामक हैं। रायपुर पुलिस ऐसी किसी घटना की पुष्टि नहीं करती है।
कृपया अफवाहों पर विश्वास न करें, न ही उन्हें साझा करें। किसी भी संदिग्ध वस्तु की सूचना तत्काल पुलिस को दें। pic.twitter.com/RE8wKJTCWr
— Police Commissionerate Raipur (@CPRaipurPolice) June 18, 2026
We then performed a reverse image search on the viral photograph of the alleged pen bomb. The same image was found on the American e-commerce website Etsy. The pen shown in the viral image is described as a military novelty tank or grenade ink pen and is listed for sale on the website for approximately ₹6,163.
According to the Etsy listing, these military-style tank and grenade pens are available in several finishes, including 24-carat gold, antique brass, gun pewter and green metal. The pens can be refilled using Parker-style ink cartridges.
The product description further states that the pen makes an ideal gift for those who serve and protect their country. The manufacturer, Penatradition, has been registered on Etsy for nine years. Nowhere in the product description is there any mention of explosives being concealed inside the pens.
In other words, the object shown in the viral image is not a real grenade or explosive device. It is a military-style grenade-themed ink pen available for purchase on Etsy.
Next, we conducted a reverse image search of the photographs showing injured individuals. Four of the images were found in a post published on June 11, 2026, by the official X account of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) Punjab.
According to the post, people coming out of a mosque after Fajr prayers in Rawalakot were fired upon. These images were actually from an incident in Rawalakot, Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK), where Pakistani forces allegedly opened fire with AK-47 rifles on thousands of unarmed protesters demanding basic rights. The incident reportedly left 16 people dead and 37 injured.
However, we were unable to determine the origin of the remaining two viral images independently. This report will be updated if new information becomes available.
To sum up, the viral claims about so-called “pen bombs” are actually a grenade-themed ink refill pen sold on Etsy. Both Bulandshahr and Raipur police confirmed that no advisory about such objects exists, and no incidents have been reported. The visuals being circulated are old and unrelated. The viral claim on social media is false and misleading.

title: Muslim girl forced to chant ‘Jai Sri Ram’? No, old Dhaka video edited to give communal angle
body_text:
A 24-second long video is being shared widely as visuals of Hindu men forcing a Muslim woman to say ‘Jai Sri Ram’.
The video shows a woman wearing a hijab crying even as a group of men intimidate and heckle her. In the background, two-three male voices can be heard saying, “Bol Jai Sri Ram” repeatedly in Bengali.
X user Mannan Pathan uploaded the video on June 21, 2026. (Archive)
Forcing a young girl to cry Jai shree Ram. This man is mercilessly man handling this kid.
Am sure now that JSR is brand terrorism slogan.
I don’t see many majoritarian objecting to such cheep act.
All want is hate across pic.twitter.com/Gs8HYvCUtJ— Mannan Pathan (@mannan_pathan) June 21, 2026
The post has gathered over 55,o00 views and has been shared more than 1,300 times at the time of this article being written. Pathan reshared a post by a user named Nasreen Khan which received close to 70,000 views before being deleted.
A Facebook user named Q Khan Akram posted the video on June 20. It has over 150,000 views and over 3700 shares. The user has not mentioned where the incident took place. His bio says he is from Ranchi, Jharkhand.
Other social media users also shared the same video, helping it go viral.
Alt News received multiple requests on its WhatsApp helpline to verify the authenticity of the video.
Fact Check
By running reverse image searches with a few key frames from the video, we were able to locate the same uploaded by Bangladeshi social media users in September 2025.
Sylhetor News, a media outlet based in Sylhet, Bangladesh, shared it on Facebook on September 16, 2025. According to the caption, a girl named Anika was caught stealing from a shop in Mirpur College Market, Dhaka. Some locals assaulted her after catching her red handed.
The footage contains fairly clear audio. ItT does not match the soundtrack of the viral video and no one can be heard raising a ‘Jai Sri Ram’ slogan.
At the 50-second mark in the video uploaded by Sylhetor News, the CCTV footage of the shop from where the girl was apprehended is shown. In that CCTV footage, the name of the store, Sohana’s Pet Shop, can be spotted.
We located the shop in Mirpur, Dhaka by a simple google map search. It is located at 76/A, Hazarat Shah Ali College Market, Dhaka.
The same video was also posted by other Facebook users such as Sobuj Anwar Hossein Byabsha, BD Tube Master News. These posts carry the same details about the incident. Several users objected to the girl being assaulted.
None of these videos contain ‘Jai Sri Ram’ slogans.
Thus it is clear that a video from Bangladesh was edited with an audio overlay to claim that a Muslim woman had been forced to chant ‘Jai Sri Ram’ slogans by Hindus in India. The original footage is from Dhaka where in September 2025 the woman was allegedly caught stealing from a shop.

title: Fact check: Viral post falsely claims all accused in Delhi ISI spy network case are Muslims
body_text:
A list naming seven individuals arrested in connection with an alleged Delhi-based spy network linked to Pakistan’s Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) is being widely shared on social media following a June 16 NDTV report on the case. The names are Anas, Mohammad, Danish, Arif, Sabir, Khalid and Junaid.
X user @Warlock_mohit amplified the claim by quoting NDTV’s post and sharing a list of the alleged names of those arrested, all of whom were presented as Muslim. The post was captioned, “Always Peacefuls” — a derogatory term frequently used in Right-wing online discourse to stereotype Muslims as violent or criminal.
The post has received over 2.27 lakh views and has been reshared over 2,800 times. (Archive)
Their names are:
Anas
Mohammad
Danish
Arif
Sabir
Khalid
JunaidAlways Peacefuls 💔 https://t.co/MtYKe5wlvv
— Mohit (@Warlock_mohit) June 17, 2026
Several other X users, such as @FabulasGuy, @AmitLeliSlayer, etc., quoted NDTV’s X post carrying the report link with the same list of names.
Fact Check
To verify this claim, we examined the NDTV report. Contrary to social media posts suggesting that all seven accused were Muslims, the report identified the arrested individuals as Anas alias Anas Tyagi, Mohit alias Yogi, Deepak alias Deepak Agrola, Arif alias Pradhan, Karanveer Singh, Jatan and Sabir. According to NDTV, the seven were allegedly linked to a network operated by Pakistan-based gangster-turned-terrorist Shahzad Bhatti and his associate Ajmal Gujjar.
The same names were was mentioned in reports by The Hindu, India Today and several other outlets.
A June 19 report by The Print mentioned that police arrested five more individuals in a second operation — Sohail, Sonu Meena, Sachin Kumar Meena, Md Kaif, and Md Rihan.
Alt News accessed the two press releases issued by the Delhi Police special cell in connection with the case. Both documents confirm the above-mentioned names. The second press release, comprising the five individuals apprehended in the second operation, also mentioned three more names who had been arrested in a previous operation — Soyab, Anmol Rai alias Annu and Ravi Kashyap. It also mentioned the family details of the five arrested recently — Sohail (26) s/o Shamshad Ali, Sonu Meena (30) s/o Mewa Lal Meena, Sachin Kumar Meena (20) s/o Devi Shaya Meena, Md Kaif (21) s/o Lallu, and Md Rihan (20) s/o Md Shakeel.
To sum up, the viral claim is false. Records related to the case show that at least five of the arrested individuals are Hindus. Moreover, neither the NDTV report nor police records mention any Danish, Khalid or Junaid among the 12 people arrested so far. The list circulating on social media therefore misrepresents the identities of the accused and seeks to give the alleged crime a communal colour.

title: No, South Korean YouTuber was not groped in India while dressed as a pregnant woman
body_text:
A 3-minute-long video is being circulated on social media, displaying a South Korean YouTuber conducting a social experiment by wearing a prosthetic belly to appear as a pregnant woman, purportedly to test women’s safety in India, as claimed by social media users.
In one of the clips, the YouTuber is seen seemingly being harassed by a group of men during Holi who forcefully apply colour on him and put their hands inside his shirt. The clip is being shared by users to highlight the dire state of women’s safety in the country.
An X user NEXTA (@nexta_tv) shared the video on June 6 and claimed that a “Chinese man decided to prove that women are perfectly safe in India and Bangladesh…According to him, he was repeatedly groped, harassed, and nearly sexually assaulted…” The post received over 3 million views and has been reposted 2,300 times. (Archive)
A Chinese man decided to prove that women are perfectly safe in India and Bangladesh
He disguised himself as an unattractive pregnant woman and spent three days in public.
According to him, he was repeatedly groped, harassed, and nearly sexually assaulted.
Now he says he has a… pic.twitter.com/w9yut9Zuk1
— NEXTA (@nexta_tv) June 6, 2026
Thai news outlet The Thaiger posted a screengrab of the alleged Holi incident on Facebook on June 6 with the text: “Korean YouTuber mistaken for a woman, groped throughout India’s Holi festival”. The caption further mentioned that the incident happened in Varanasi during Holi.
Several other pages and users on social media shared the video, claiming that the social experiment done by the South Korean YouTuber exposed the plight of women’s safety in India. Below are a few more instances of the same video being circulated:
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of this claim, we first broke down the viral video into several keyframes and ran a reverse image search on a few of them. This led us to a page on a South Korean web portal called Daum, which carried screengrabs from the original YouTube video posted by the content creator. We noticed that the channel’s name, “I am Sumin”.
We found the original video of him dressing up as a pregnant woman on his YouTube channel. The video was posted on April 11, 2026. At the 1:00-minute mark, the content creator mentions in the video that he was filming this in Bangladesh. The video did not feature India or any Holi celebration.
In another video, posted on the channel on May 12, 2025, titled, “India’s Largest Hindu Festival, ‘Holi Festival’ [India] 7 Varanasi”, the viral clip from Holi appears. At the 3:16-minute mark, the viral part of the video begins, and Sumin is playing and enjoying Holi with locals in Varanasi. Notably, Sumin appears as himself, visiting Varanasi to participate in the Holi festival. Thus, the viral video was created by editing two completely different clips to create a false narrative.
Later, a comment made by Sumin himself under a now-deleted post on Threads clarifies that the claims being associated with his videos depicting him being harassed in India are false. He reaffirms that he was indeed enjoying playing Holi and Varanasi as himself and further calls out the fabrication of an “anti-India” narrative as “baseless” and “malicious”
Thus, the claim that the YouTuber was harassed in India during Holi while he cross-dressed as a pregnant woman is entirely false. The video of him cross-dressing is from Bangladesh, and the Holi video is from last year and does not show him appearing as a woman or being harassed.

title: Did Amit Shah say JAAC would help India retrieve PoK? Viral video manipulated
body_text:
A video clip displaying Union home minister Amit Shah stating that the Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) would assist India in retrieving Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) is being widely circulated on social media platforms.
Founded in 2023, the JAAC is reportedly a grassroots coalition and socio-political organisation of civil-society members in PoK.
In the video, Shah states that PoK is part of India, thus both Hindus and Muslims residing there are Indians. Then the video appears to show Shah saying, “The JAAC will help us get back PoK”.
A Pakistan-based Facebook user, Amir Hassan Qureshi, shared the purported clip claiming that Shah had explicitly stated that “the Joint Awami Action Committee will play a role in India’s occupation of Azad Kashmir.”
On June 7, 2026, the same person made a similar post on X.
Another X user, Shirmeen Khurram, who identifies herself as the associate director of MKT Express Media, further shared the clip alleging that Shah had ‘openly declared’ that Awami Action Committee will ‘play a role in facilitating India’s occupation of Azad Kashmir’.
Further, an Instagram user, Mateen Ahmed Abbasi, shared a similar video asserting that Shah had admitted that the JAAC is ‘fomenting chaos in Azad Kashmir at the behest of India’.
Fact Check
Upon closely examining the video, we noticed that the microphone Shah is holding has “India Today Conclave 2024” written on it.
Based on that finding, we conducted a simple keyword search on YouTube, leading us to the original video that was published first on March 15, 2024, on the official India Today Conclave page.
In the video, starting at the 33:26 timestamp, Shah addresses PoK, declaring it as part of India. He states, “Pak-occupied Kashmir is a part of India. There is no question about Hindu or Muslim. All the Muslims and Hindus residing there are ours (Indians).”
Following this, journalist Rahul Kanwal highlights the significance of Shah’s statement. The home minister then interjects, “I have said this in Parliament as well, and I will say it again. Pak-occupied Kashmir is a part of India. The Muslims and Hindus there are ours,” prompting applause from the audience.
Shah did not make any other statements regarding JAAC or suggest that it would play a role in India reclaiming PoK.
Additionally, we found the manipulated clip, which first went viral, uploaded as a short by Business Today on YouTube.
Further, there are no credible reports or any official statement by Shah solidifying this claim.
Conclusively, the video clip claiming that Shah stated that JAAC would help India to retrieve PoK is inauthentic. The original video has been digitally manipulated, with fabricated synthetic audio.

title: No, viral Ujjain video does not show Muslim men being punished for harassing girls
body_text:
A video, around 37 seconds long, is viral on social media. It shows three youths bound with ropes being assaulted by a man carrying a cane. Police personnel can be seen present at the scene, as the three young men are paraded down the street.
Several users have amplified this clip, claiming that the three young men — all of them Muslims — were supposedly apprehended and beaten up for harassing schoolgirls.
X user @HindutvaDon_ posted the clip on X. The post amassed more than 50,000 views and close to 1,000 re-shares at the time of this article being written. He claimed the three were Mohammad Arif, Asraf Ali and Asif Khan. (Archive)
X user @MeghUpdates, who frequently shares communal misinformation, posted the clip with the same claim — that the three perpetrators were Muslims. (Archive)
Arif, Ashraf, and Asif used to harass and evetease girls outside a college. They were caught by Police, tied with ropes, publicly paraded on the street, and serviced with lathis; Video of the swift action is now going viral. pic.twitter.com/F4mDBpqx8W
— Megh Updates 🚨™ (@MeghUpdates) June 12, 2026
X user (@RealBababanaras) had uploaded this clip, back on May 26, with a similar claim. (Archive)
Meet Mohammad Sameer, Mohammad Nadeem & Mohammad Asif Ali.
Their primary job was harassing the girl students outside the college.
They had made the lives of the girls going to and from school a living hell.
But the police did great job
What punishment should be given to them pic.twitter.com/hbfotK8hzq
— Baba Banaras™ (@RealBababanaras) May 26, 2026
Readers should note that Baba Banaras, too, has been found sharing communal misinformation several times in the past.
Several other users, including @Sudhir_mish, @SouleFacts and @ocjain4 also circulated the claims. Archives: 1, 2, 3
Fact Check
To investigate the veracity of the claims surrounding this viral video, we broke it down into keyframes. A reverse image search on one such keyframe led us to this X post, made by user @IacHead, on April 17.
#उज्जैन में रात के समय कुछ लोगों ने गुंडागर्दी करते हुए मकानों और गाड़ियों पर पथराव किया, जिससे कई गाड़ियों और खिड़कियों को नुकसान पहुंचा।पुलिस ने कार्रवाई करते हुए तीन आरोपियों किशन, विकास और रमेश को गिरफ्तार कर लिया। साथ ही, उन्हें उसी स्थान पर ले जाया गया जहां उन्होंने यह… pic.twitter.com/GYcFQK9OXF
— INDIA APRADH CONTROL NEWS . अख़बार (@IacHead) April 17, 2026
It is the same video as the one that went viral recently. The caption, however, contradicts the claims, identifying the three accused as Kishan, Vikas, and Ramesh. It is said that they had been beaten up for vandalising houses and windows in the neighbourhood at night.
Taking cue from the caption of this X post, we ran a relevant keyword search on Google. This led us to an article by Dainik Bhaskar, published on April 16, which reports on the incident.
According to the report, late into the night on April 16, some 10-12 miscreants, armed with sticks, knives and swords, pelted stones to vandalise houses windows and vehicles in the Dhanda Bhavan area of Ujjain, in Madhya Pradesh. Police arrested three individuals and paraded them in the area. The report also mentions the name of the three accused who appear in the viral video: Kishan Ahirwar, Vikas Saroniya, and Vishal.
Another report, by Navbharat Times, elucidates that the dispute had broken over something as trivial as a refusal to offer a car wash. The complainant, one Sheran Ali, had declined a car wash to the miscreants, citing time constraint. This resulted in a violent retaliation, culminating in the vandalism of vehicles and house windows late at night.
In conclusion, the viral video, which several users have claimed shows three Muslim youths accused of harassing schoolgirls, actually shows three miscreants in Ujjain who vandalised private property, in April 2026. They are not Muslims. Their names are Kishan Ahirwar, Vikas Saroniya, and Vishal.

title: Patna video shared by BJP: Is it real or AI generated?
body_text:
A video, around 12 seconds long, was posted on X on June 4 from the official handle of the Bhartiya Janata Party (BJP), purporting to show development under the BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) government in Patna. (Archive)
चौंकिए मत, ये बिहार की राजधानी पटना है!
जहां भाजपा-NDA सरकार,
वहां विकास की तेज रफ्तार…⬇️ pic.twitter.com/MLH6KMAIn2— BJP (@BJP4India) June 4, 2026
Ravi Shankar Prasad, a BJP Member of Parliament from Patna, also posted the clip on X, where it amassed more than 2,000,000 views at the time of this article being written, besides being shared more than 1,000 times. (Archive)
सुंदर पटना – विकसित पटना
भाजपा-NDA का वादा, एकदम पक्का pic.twitter.com/WjMcqP4UiF— Ravi Shankar Prasad (@rsprasad) June 3, 2026
The clip was amplified with the same claim by several other state units of the BJP, including those of Bihar, Arunachal Pradesh, and Jharkhand. Archives: 1, 2, 3
Several BJP leaders, including the likes of Gopal Jee Thakur, Lal Singh Arya, Vivek Thakur, and others. Archives: 1, 2, 3
Screenshots below:
On June 4, the official X handle of the Indian National Congress called out the viral clip, labelling it an “AI-generated fake video.”
नरेंद्र मोदी के मार्गदर्शन में BJP ने AI से बना हुआ एक फर्जी वीडियो पोस्ट किया।
लोगों ने इनकी चालबाजी पकड़ ली और अब वीडियो डिलीट हो गया है।
झांसेबाज मोदी की खोखली इमेज ऐसे ही झूठ पर टिकी है, लेकिन अब इनका फर्जियापा चलने वाला नहीं। pic.twitter.com/eATVoOKujp
— Congress (@INCIndia) June 4, 2026
Fact Check
We noted that BJP’s Bihar unit had responded to the allegation made by the Congress. They uploaded a comparison video, adding clips from the real locations (namely, Lohia Path Chakra, Atal Path, JP Ganga Path) at daytime, which correspond to the locations shown in the viral clip.
AI? Asli India है ये! PM Modi का New India है ये!
यहाँ राजद-कांग्रेस राज वाले फ़र्ज़ीवाड़े नहीं होते, यहाँ सपने पूरे होते हैं। यहाँ विकास होता है।
अब भी यकीन ना हुआ हो, तो:
– लोहिया पथ चक्र
– अटल पथ
– जेपी गंगा पथघूम जाइए, नए बिहार और नए बिहार के नए विश्वास पर गर्व… https://t.co/mbKP7AN4Kq pic.twitter.com/Ugrg0mTqvI
— BJP Bihar (@BJP4Bihar) June 5, 2026
On further investigation, we were able to geolocate the precise locations which are purportedly shown in the video uploaded by the BJP.
The first location, as shown in the comparison below, can be found here.
The second site, showing an overhead bridge, could be geolocated here.
Likewise, the third location could be found here.
The fourth and final location represented in the clip could be found here.
However, on going through the clip closely, we noticed several irregularities.
The Hindi text on the signboard, which appears in the first few sections of the video, does not make sense. Moreover, the movements of the couple seen walking on the left side of the road around the 0:04 timestamp appear garbled, and they seem to be walking backwards. At the 0:09 timestamp, one of the motorcycles on the highway disappear into thin air.
Another glaring discrepancy was the absence of the extravagant lighting on the highway structures. It is important to note here that the video comparison uploaded by @BJP4Bihar on June 5 was shot during daytime, and hence it does not throw any light on the decorative and bright white lightings shown in the viral clip.
An Alt News representative conducted a nighttime visit to the four locations in Patna and recorded videos to assess whether the visuals in the clip accurately reflect the ground reality. Check the comparisons below:
This investigation shows that the structures shown in the viral clip, which on first look appear to be AI-generated, are very much real. However, that does not discount the fact that several discrepancies occur throughout the viral clip, including irregularities in object-rendering and text.
Most importantly, the videographic evidence we collected from the sites clearly demonstrate that none of the structures or roadways are lit up in the manner in which they are represented in the viral clip, meaning that real inputs of these locations across Patna have been artificially embellished by AI, exaggerating the lights in particular, to generate the clip in question.
We also ran the viral clip through Hive’s AI detection tool. According to the platform, there was a 99.4% possibility of the clip containing AI-generated content.
On further investigation, we were able to trace the origins of the AI-enhanced video to a creator named Ujjal Singh (@uujjwwall) on Instagram. The user had uploaded the viral clip on June 2, a couple of days prior to its amplification by the BJP on social media.
View this post on Instagram
The user had uploaded a comparison between this AI-moderated clip and the original video, showing clearly how AI had enhanced the lighting and the shadows.
View this post on Instagram
So, to conclude, this demonstrates that feeding real photographs or videos into an AI system can generate enhanced or distorted representations, even when the structures depicted are grounded in real-world details. Although such content can not be technically called AI-generated, (since it is not “created” artificially but is merely “modified” from existing footage), that does not change the fact that the resulting representation may no longer accurately reflect reality.

title: Abhishek Banerjee threatened to kill BJP supporters? No; video clipped, context distorted
body_text:
A seven-second video is doing rounds on the social media, where TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee allegedly threatens to assault BJP supporters once the Opposition Bloc, INDIA, comes to power.
In the viral clip, Banerjee can be heard saying, “Je dinke Mamata Bandyopadhyay aar INDIA Alliance er Sarkar hobey nah, baritey dhukey maarbo”. (The day Mamata Bandyopadhyay and INDIA Alliance’s government is formed, we will enter (their) houses and beat (them).”
X user Gourab posted the video on May 31, 2026. (Archive)
Remembering that famous THREAT of Criminal, Chor Drama-Artist Abhishek Banerjee (Kingpin of infamous Diamond Harbour Model) -when he loudly asserted to “Thrash & Murder (BJP Supporters) by entering their houses, the day when Mamata Banerjee & INDI alliance’s Govt will be formed”… pic.twitter.com/t176TD9qhi
— Gourab | গৌরব | (@gourabsays) May 30, 2026
Alt News received multiple requests to authenticate the video.
Trinamool national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee was assaulted by a mob while he was on his way to visit the family of a TMC worker allegedly murdered by BJP activists in Sonarpur, on May 30. Once video clips and news of that incident started surfacing, this video, where Abhishek Banerjee is apparently seen threatening his political opponents, also started to gather steam.
The same X user, Gourab, posted this video while commenting to an X post by TMC spokesperson Tanmoy Ghosh on May 30 condemning the attack on Abhishek Banerjee.
কিরে চোর-ডাকাতদের মুখপাত্র ;
Remember that famous Threat of Ur Master Abhishek Banerjee (The Kingpin of infamous Diamond Harbour Model) on – How he will “kill & murder BJP supporters, leaders by entering their houses” !!
Today Karma showed him Mirror 🪞pic.twitter.com/9C28BEuTCo
— Gourab | গৌরব | (@gourabsays) May 30, 2026
Many other social media users across various platforms also started to share the video in question, making it go viral.
Fact Check
A simple reverse image search using a few keyframes of the video led us to an YouTube shorts posted by the Bengali News Channel R Plus.
The clip is from one of Abhishek Banerjee’s election rallies in Malda. On that day he campaigned for the TMC candidates of Harishchandrapur and Chanchal in Malda district.
In this 2.23-minute-long video, Banerjee claims that people sitting in Pakistan and Bangladesh threatens to destroy Delhi and Kolkata, while BJP and Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah sits idly. He further alleges that Pakistani defence Minister Khwaja Asif Muneer himself threatened to destroy Delhi and Kolkata.
Then he went on to say, “I am writing down Khwaja Asif’s name. The day Mamata Bandyopadhyay and and INDIA Alliance forms government, we will target him in his own house”.
This part of the speech can be heard between the 28 second to 38 second-mark in the video. In the now viral clip, the first part of the statement has been cropped out.
Furthermore, Abhishek’s threat to the Pakistani Defence Minister was also covered by Bengali News portals and news channels like Ei Samay and ABP Ananda.
Thus it is evident that a portion of Abhishek Banerjee’s speech is now getting viral with the false claim that he threatened his political opponents.

title: Month-old video falsely viral as Hindu girl raped in front of father in Bangladesh
body_text:
This story uses only screenshots and not the actual video in view of its graphic nature.
A 28-second video is viral on social media where a man and a woman can be seen crying inconsolably. Users claim that the woman, a Bangladeshi Hindu, was brutally raped in front of her father. The video captures both of them purportedly breaking down as she recounted the harrowing incident. The video also shows the woman displaying her torn clothes while saying, “Look at my situation, see what they have done with me” in Bengali.
Radharamn Das, vice-president of ISKCON Kolkata, posted the video on May 29, 2026. His post was viewed 2,34,000 times and reposted 4,900 times at the time of writing. (Archive)
Several X users shared the video with the same claim.
Fact Check
The reader must note that at no point in the video are either the woman or the man heard mentioning rape.
Alt News ran a reverse image search of some keyframes of the viral video. This search led us to a fact-check article dated May 24, containing the same video. Published by a Bangladeshi web portal called ‘Bangla Stream‘, the report stated that the video, originally from Comilla in Bangladesh, was being falsely shared across Facebook, with the claim that it was filmed after a rape incident in Barishal.
The fact-check report clarified that it was not, in fact, a rape. The woman, Rina Rani Saha, seen in the video with her husband, had borrowed 80,000 Tk from an NGO named ‘Pally Mangal Karmasuchi’. They had cleared six instalments of the loan. However, when they failed to repay three instalments, they were assaulted by recovery agents of the NGO.
Another Comilla-based web portal called Comilla Pratidin also covered the incident in a report dated May 15. According to the report, the incident took place on April 30 near the Goalpatty area of Comilla City. The husband, Shyamal Chandra Sarkar, had lodged a written complaint at the Comilla Kotwali Model Police Station a few hours after the incident.
The complaint named four people as the accused — Miraj Hossain, Rahid, Iti Debnath and Roksana Akhtar. Incidentally, one of the accused, Iti Debnath, is also Hindu. This implies that the attack could not have been communal.
Sarkar alleged that Debnath and Akhtar assaulted his wife.
Furthermore, a relevant keyword search led us to several related Facebook posts by Bangladeshi news portals. In a clip of an interview with the victim uploaded by Facebook page Dainik Sokal, the victim talks about how four NGO employees assaulted her over the non-payment of a loan. The same details were reported by a Facebook page named Drishtikon, which also uploaded the viral video.
A month-old video from Bangladesh is being falsely shared by Indian social media users as visuals of a Hindu girl and her father breaking down in front of the camera after she was allegedly raped in front of him. In reality, the video shows a couple who were assaulted over the non-repayment of a loan, and one of the accused is Hindu.

title: Viral video of SRH owner Kavya Maran in tears after SRH loss is two years old, not recent
body_text:
A video, showing Kavya Maran, co-owner and CEO of the IPL team Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH), is viral on social media, where she is seen breaking down in tears, followed by clapping. According to social media users, Maran was reportedly crying after SRH lost the crucial eliminator clash against the Rajasthan Royals on May 27, which resulted in their exit from the tournament.
Posting the clip, several users pointed out that Kavya Maran’s team, Sunrisers Leeds, from the UK’s The Hundred cricket league, had signed Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed in March this year — a move that had sparked controversy and drawn criticism from former India captain Sunil Gavaskar, among others. It is to be noted that the IPL had not featured any Pakistani cricketer since the 2008 Mumbai terror attacks, until Ahmed’s inclusion in the Sun Group-owned franchise.
X user (@smitadeshmukh) posted the clip on May 28, writing that she “felt for the team”, but could not discount the fact that its owner had bought a Pakistani cricketer who had insulted the Indian armed forces. Since then, the post has gathered more than 82,000 views. (Archive)
I feel for the team, but can’t forget the owner who kept on pitching for a Pak player, who has insulted our armed forces, for her Hundred franchise. Cry as much, but glad to see her out.
Go to UK for Hundred. 😡 pic.twitter.com/i6sNxvHPDb— Smita Deshmukh🇮🇳 (@smitadeshmukh) May 28, 2026
Several X users, like (@Mamtasulaniya), (@drona_17), (@varunavi17), (@Santosh13298329) and others also circulated the viral clip, claiming that it was shot after SRH suffered their exit from this year’s edition of the IPL. Archives: 1, 2, 3, 4
“Maha Sports”, a Marathi sports news outlet, also posted the viral clip on Facebook and Instagram, where it has seen significant traction. At the time os writing this article, the post had gathered around 8 lakh views on Facebook, and close to 9 lakh views on Instagram.
View this post on Instagram
Fact Check
To check the veracity of the viral claim, we broke down the video into multiple keyframes. A reverse image search on the viral keyframe led us to this article, by the Hindustan Times, published on May 27, 2024.
The report reads that Kavya Maran had broken down into tears following her team’s loss in the final match, against the Kolkata Knight Riders, on May 26, 2024, at Chennai. She was also spotted applauding the rival team, congratulating them on their second title win.
We also found reports from other news outlets, like India Today, Times of India, News18, Live Mint, and others, which corroborated our findings.
Thereafter, we checked to ensure that the jersey Kavya Maran dons in the viral clip was the official IPL jersey of the SRH in the year 2024.
We also found a video posted by Sunrisers Hyderabad on their YouTube channel, which showed Kavya Maran consoling the team in the dressing room moments after their loss in the final match in 2024. The jersey is the same as the one she wears in the viral clip.
We also checked out SRH’s official jersey from this year’s IPL, and found that it was different.
The official Instagram handle of SRH had posted the post-match scenes from the crucial Eliminator clash, which also featured Kavya Maran, wearing a jersey different from the one seen in the viral video.
View this post on Instagram
In conclusion, the viral clip, which shows Kavya Maran breaking down into tears, was taken shortly after SRH had lost in the final to the Kolkata Knight Riders in 2024. It is not from this year’s IPL, as the viral claims suggest.

title: No, the man in this viral confession video is not Asad: the clip is from a Delhi stabbing in April 2026
body_text:
A video is being circulated on social media where a man can be seen confessing to a murder. Users have claimed that the man in the video is Asad, the prime accused in the murder of 17-year-old Surya Pratap Chauhan in Ghaziabad.
Chauhan was stabbed in Ghaziabad’s Khoda area on May 28, the day of Eid al-Adha, allegedly by Asad and his associates following an argument. According to the victim’s family, the two sides knew each other and had been involved in a dispute some months prior. The victim’s sister claimed Surya was lured to the spot on the pretext of an Eid celebration. “He was at home when he received a call asking him to come and celebrate Eid. He did not want to go, but they kept insisting, so he eventually left with them,” she said, adding that seven people were present and that all of them were carrying knives. Asad was shot dead in a police encounter on May 31. Three others have been arrested in connection with the case, including Asad’s father.
X user @Ridhu417Sharma amplified the video, saying, “This is India’s scared Muslim, who are posting videos after committing murder.”
अशद यह है भारत का डरा हुआ मुस्लिम यह मर्डर करने के बाद वीडियो डाल रहे है। 🥹
अब भी सड़कछाप कौमी लोग जो बोल रहे थे की हमे पता नहीं लो तुम्हारी औलाद ने ख़ुद बता दिया की कितने नीच है यह।🥹 pic.twitter.com/2LozBQoz9K— SanataniRiddhi (@Ridhu417Sharma) May 31, 2026
Several others posted the video on X with the same claim.
Some users also shared the viral video on Facebook.
Fact Check
A reverse image search of a keyframe from the viral video led us to an Instagram post from April 5. The caption identified the clip as Karan alias Tarun, 18, confessing to the stabbing and killing of a 20-year-old named Nitesh, also known as Nikki, in Delhi’s Shalimar Bagh area. Police said the attack stemmed from a dispute and that Karan had carried it out along with his associates. He was subsequently arrested.
Вижте тази публикация в Instagram.
This was also reported at the time by mainstream media outlets such as Times Now, NDTV, and India Today. According to NDTV’s report, Karan Kumar and two other people, including a juvenile, stabbed Nitesh following an argument in the Shalimar Bagh police station area in North West Delhi on April 2. Nitesh was taken to a hospital, where he was declared brought dead. Following the incident, Kumar took to his Instagram, under the handle ‘villain_dad_ok_109’, to inform his 1,500 followers that he had killed someone (“tumhare bhai ne murder maar diya hai”).
Kumar said in the Instagram story, “Dekho bhai, raat ke baj rahe hai 11.22 pm. Tumhare bhai ne ek murder maar diya hai. Tumhara bhai kuch din ke liye andar ja raha hai. To saare bhai pyaar dena, hain? (It’s 11:22 PM. Your brother has committed a murder, and he is going away for a few days, okay? So, show your brother some love),” he said.
We looked up the handle ‘villain_dad_ok_109‘ mentioned in the viral video. The Instagram profile also lists the username as Karan. Kumar also has a YouTube channel.
As it stands, the claims associated with the video circulating online linking it with the accused in the Surya Pratap Chauhan case are misleading. It is actually from approximately two months ago, where a young Karan Kumar, alias Tarun, stabbed a 20-year-old Nitesh in Delhi and confessed it in a video uploaded to his Instagram handle.

title: Video of Jaishankar requesting US to hand over Cockroach Janata Party founder to India is a deepfake
body_text:
A minute-long video is doing rounds on the social media, where Indian Foreign Minister S Jaishankar is heard saying, despite India being a close ally, the US administration is sheltering Cockroach Janta Party(CJP)’s leadership. He is even heard requesting the US to hand CJP leader Abhijeet Dipke to India, as he is in cahoots with Pakistani and Iranian proxy groups.
The video started to circulate while Marco Rubio was on an official visit to India. CJI Surya Kant made remarks during a Supreme Court hearing in which he compared certain individuals who had entered the legal profession via fake degrees, and then taken to activism and social media, to cockroaches. The remarks were widely interpreted as targeting unemployed youth and activists more generally, though the CJI later issued a clarification. In reaction to his comment, an online movement came into existence, called the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP). The movement grew rapidly and within days it garnered millions of followers, eclipsing the social media follower count of BJP across platforms. Unable to tackle the situation, the official X page of the movement was withheld in India. The founder of this movement, Abhijeet Dipke is currently based in the US. In this context, the video is being shared, where S Jaishankar is seen virtually pleading to the US to stop sheltering persons who are an inconvenience to the Indian Government.
In the viral video, Jaishankar is seen standing on a podium and heard saying, “I also have to put forward a request, some non-state actors in the United States are using America as a launch platform for Anti-India movements, the most recent being the Cockroach Janata Party. I never expected the US to host individuals like Abhijeet Dipke, who has contacts with the Iranian Ayatollah regime, and are running a campaign on their behalf. While India stayed loyal to the USA and Israel and left our relation with Iran, the US soil kept hosting the Cockroach Janata Party. I want to request honorable President Trump to hand over the incumbent leadership of Cockroach Janata Party, as they are acting as proxies for Iran and Pakistan. They should be handed over to India”.
X user ‘The Whistle Blower’ posted the video on 25 May 2026, and it has more than 50,000 views and 79 shares till date. (Archive) Fawad Hussain, a former minister in Pakistan’s PTI government, quote-tweeted the video, lending it additional amplification across the India-Pakistan information space.
Some other X users also posted the same video with similar claims, amplifying the reach of the video.
Fact Check
At the 30-31 second mark of the video, there is an unnatural lip sync which clearly directs towards an adulterated video. Once we became sure that this is a deepfake video, we started looking for the original video which was used to create the fake version.
In the video Jaishankar is seen reminding the US government, how ‘loyal’ they are to the Trump administration. Also Jaishankar is seen speaking from a podium, with Indian National Flags in the background. We took it as a clue, and started looking for videos where Jaishankar spoke with a foreign counterpart, in this case, an US Statesman.
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited India from May 23- May 26, 2026, and he held bilateral talks with Indian Foreign Minister S Jaishankar on May 24. As the video in question was posted on May 25, it was a clue that footage from that bilateral meet could have been used to produce the deepfake. A little online search landed us onto the You Tube channel of India’s Ministry of External Affairs, which had streamed the entire bilateral session. The video is 52 minutes and 53 seconds long.
And in the almost hour long video, S Jaishankar never uttered a single word about CJP, or requesting the US Government to hand over individuals such as Abhijeet Dipke. The transcript of the session can be read here.
Furthurmore, the podium, the alignment of the Indian National flags and even the colour of Jaishankar’s shirt as seen in the deepfake matched exactly with the setup of this video.
Along with that, Press Information Bureau posted a fact check on this video on May 25 itself, where they clearly mentioned that this video is a deepfake.
🚨 Deepfake Alert!
👉🏻 Propaganda accounts are circulating a digitally altered video of External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar claiming that he has requested US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and President Donald Trump to hand over the handlers of Cockroach Janta Party to… pic.twitter.com/3oVBcl4Mox
— PIB Fact Check (@PIBFactCheck) May 25, 2026
In conclusion it can be said that S Jaishankar’s video from Marco Rubio’s bilateral session was doctored and a deepfake video was made where he requests the US Government to hand over Abhijeet Dipke to India.

title: Video of Dhaka eviction drive falsely claimed as BJP bulldozer action against ‘Bangladeshis’ in Bengal
body_text:
A 41-second video has been circulating on social media, claiming the BJP government in West Bengal is demolishing illegal slums, which were occupied by Bangladeshi infiltrators. The viral video shows a group of people wailing uncontrollably as the sound of destruction plays in the background.
X user Jitendra Pratap Singh, whose bio says “Blessed to be followed by shri @narendramodi ji PM of India”, posted the video on May 21, 2026. The video has more than 400,000 views and 4000 reshares till date. (Archive)
पश्चिम बंगाल में बांग्लादेशियों की बस्ती पर बुलडोजर एक्शन हो रहा है
यह सब वन विभाग और सरकारी जमीन पर बसे थे
वीडियो में पीछे देखिए एक व्यक्ति तो बांग्लादेश क्रिकेट टीम के जर्सी में खड़ा है pic.twitter.com/IkFXyfIZ3c
— 🇮🇳Jitendra pratap singh🇮🇳 (@jpsin1) May 21, 2026
The caption of the video, translated from Hindi, says: “Bangladeshi slums in West Bengal are witnessing Bulldozer action. All these slums were built on the encroached lands of the forest department and other government departments. One guy can be seen in the video wearing the jersey of Bangladesh Cricket Team”.
A Facebook user called ‘Pralay Mondal’ also shared the same clip on May 22, 2026. This video has 1400 reactions and 39,000 views till now.
The same video was shared by several other social media users, between May 21 and May 22, which helped it to go viral.
Fact Check
While playing the video, at 35-36 second mark, at the bottom right corner of the video, one can see a boom used by the news media. The boom belongs to ‘Kaler Kantho’- a major Bengali-language daily newspaper.
Also at the 28 second mark, the video shows the hoarding of a shop called ‘Khan Bastro Bitan’. The hoarding contains a name- Abdul Kuddus Khan, and a contact number. The first two digits of the contact number is 01, which is the country code for Bangladesh for in-country calling.
Along with that, the hoarding of ‘Khan Bastro Bitan’ mentions Dhaka, Mirpur-1216, which is the postal code for the Mirpur suburb of Dhaka, the capital of Bangladesh. Just beneath the hoarding, a youth can be seen standing, wearing the jersey of Bangladesh Cricket Team.
As we became sure that this video is from Bangladesh, we did a keyword search on Google, using the keywords- ‘Home demolished eviction drive Bangladesh family in tears’. We typed this keyword in Bengali and came across a video posted by a Facebook-based news portal ‘Sobar Kantha’.
‘Sobar Kantha‘ posted the now viral video on their Facebook page on May 20, 2026. The caption of the video says: Entire family breaks down in tears as their house is demolished.
‘Sobar Kantha’ posted a series of short videos covering the May 20 incident. The unconsolable man seen in the viral video is also seen in another video posted on that day, further proving that the now viral video is indeed from Bangladesh, showing the plight of the people whose properties are getting demolished in the name of illegal eviction drive.
In another video, which more or less covers the same moments of the now viral video, the address of ‘Khan Bastro Bitan’ can be read very clearly. The address of the shop, written in Bengali, reads as: Bauniabandh Tinshed Colony Bazar, Mirpur, Dhaka- 1216.
Bauniabandh is a locality in the Mirpur suburb of Dhaka.
Additionally, using reverse image search, we came across the same video, published by an You Tube Channel called ‘Dhaka Teligram’ on May 20, 2026.
Bangladesh based Ekattor TV also covered the news. According to their news report, a scuffle broke out between the police and the slum dwellers of Bauniabandh area of Mirpur, Dhaka, on May 20, as the police tried to demolish structures without issuing prior eviction notices. This information corroborates with the address seen on the hoarding of ‘Khan Bastro Bitan’.
In conclusion, a video from Bangladesh is circulating on social media, falsely claiming to represent demolition of illegal Bangladeshi slums in West Bengal.

title: Convoy controversy: Rajasthan MP’s cavalcade video falsely linked to BJP, PM Modi
body_text:
A 21-second viral clip showing a long convoy of cars moving along a road is being widely shared on social media with the claim that it shows a political convoy in Rajasthan, where the BJP is in power. The video has gone viral in the backdrop of PM Narendra Modi’s May 10 appeal urging citizens to use petrol, diesel and cooking gas with “great restraint” amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.
In his address, Modi also suggested measures such as using public transport, carpooling and reviving the work-from-home culture to reduce fuel consumption. He further advised people to postpone foreign travel and gold purchases for at least a year.
Several social media users and critics of the BJP shared the viral clip to accuse the Prime Minister of hypocrisy, alleging that he appealed for fuel conservation while travelling the very next day with a massive convoy of vehicles.
Among those amplifying the claim was Dhruv Rathee, who posted a video on X on May 12 criticising PM Modi for allegedly not practising what he preached. At the 36-second mark of the video, Rathee plays the viral clip and remarks that the Prime Minister’s convoy did not include any electric vehicles (EVs), which, according to him, would have reduced energy consumption. The post has garnered over 511,500 views and more than 10,000 reposts. (Archive)
Modi’s DOGLA Advice to Indians pic.twitter.com/Wpfd1Wg3b7
— Dhruv Rathee (@dhruv_rathee) May 12, 2026
Congress media coordinator from Assam, Harmeet Kaur K, also shared the viral clip in response to another post and claimed that the video showed PM Narendra Modi’s convoy in Rajasthan. (Archive)
So did their leader after appealing to their countrymen to drastically measure to conserve fuel & oil , did back to back unnecessary road shows , rallies with more than 50 car convoys, wasted foreign trips to different countries in private jets ??
And why did he make such a big… pic.twitter.com/K90xvtYM3o— Harmeet Kaur K 🇮🇳 (@iamharmeetK) May 12, 2026
Some other social media users shared the same video and claimed that it showed a convoy of Madhya Pradesh BJP leader Saubhagya Singh Thakur travelling from Ujjain to Bhopal. Some asserted that the convoy included 700-800 vehicles.
X page named BIG TV Breaking News (@bigtvtelugu) posted the viral clip on May 13, claiming that the video shows BJP leader Saubhagya Singh’s convoy. (Archive)
50 కార్లతో బీజేపీ నేత కాన్వాయ్
మధ్యప్రదేశ్ బీజేపీ నేత సౌభాగ్య సింగ్ నిర్వాకం
టెక్స్ట్ బుక్స్ కార్పొరేషన్ ఛైర్మన్గా ఉన్న సౌభాగ్య సింగ్
బాధ్యతల స్వీకరణ కోసం వెళ్లే క్రమంలో ఉజ్జయిని నుంచి భోపాల్కు 50 కార్లతో కాన్వాయ్
ఇంధనం వాడకం తగ్గించాలని ప్రధాని మోదీ చెప్పిన మరుసటి రోజే… pic.twitter.com/YJTCXQHiEM
— BIG TV Breaking News (@bigtvtelugu) May 13, 2026
Several other users shared the above-mentioned clip while targeting BJP, including Congress national spokesperson Dr. Shama Mohamed. Below are a few instances.
Fact Check
Upon closely examining the viral clip, we noticed that a person travelling in one of the vehicles in the convoy was holding a green-coloured flag. Although the footage is blurry, a white circular emblem with a logo could be seen at the centre of the flag. This suggested that the convoy was unlikely to be associated with any BJP leader.
To further verify the claim, we extracted several keyframes from the viral clip and performed a reverse image search on them. This led us to an X post by user Lala (@FabulasGuy), who had shared a screenshot of the original Instagram post from which the now-viral video appears to have originated.
Dhruv Rathee has nothing original of his own anymore.
Now he just puts into his videos whatever goes viral on Twitter/X.
This video is actually from two days ago and belongs to Rajasthan’s RLP party
Which he is falsely presenting as PM Modi’s convoy.
We are very well… pic.twitter.com/vsLi5Yrwpm
— Lala (@FabulasGuy) May 12, 2026
Following this lead, we located the Instagram page @rocky_sou_jat, where the original video had been posted on May 10 with the caption: “Beniwal’s convoy en route to Shri Dadupalka Bhairana Dham.” The post contained the same clip that is now viral.
The caption makes it clear that the convoy belonged to Hanuman Beniwal, founder of the Rashtriya Loktantrik Party (RLP) and MP from Nagaur, Rajasthan. It is worth noting that RLP split from NDA in December 2020 over farm laws.
View this post on Instagram
Armed with these clues, when we compared the flag seen in the viral video with the RLP flag, we noticed similarities.
Next, we found that the same Instagram user had shared another video in which Beniwal could be seen inside his car.
View this post on Instagram
Looking at the background of the above video, we could deduce that the viral clip and the aforementioned video are from the same place. Below, we have marked the similarities.
A similar video of a long convoy of vehicles was also shared on Hanuman Beniwal’s official Instagram page. The caption said, “On Sunday, I participated in the movement being carried out by saints and great personalities at Shri Dadupalka Bhairana Dham in Bhairana, located in Maujmadabad tehsil of Jaipur district”.
View this post on Instagram
As per reports, saints and local residents of Bhairana Dham, a prominent religious site in Rajasthan’s Behror region, have been protesting against a proposed project by the Rajasthan State Industrial Development and Investment Corporation Ltd., the Rajasthan government’s industrial development agency. Protesters allege that the project would result in the felling of around 40,000 trees.
Amid the ongoing protests, the RLP leader travelled to Bhairana Dham along with several supporters and devotees to express solidarity with the saints and residents opposing the project.
Therefore, it is clear that the viral video does not show PM Modi or any BJP leader’s convoy; it depicts RLP leader Hanuman Beniwal’s convoy, and the video is from Rajasthan.
At the same time, it is true that BJP leader Saubhagya Singh has been show-caused by the MP government for using a 50-car convoy while travelling from Ujjain to Bhopal.

title: Fake video: Clip showing Suvendu Adhikari talking about ‘finishing’ Muslims is AI generated
body_text:
A video of West Bengal chief minister Suvendu Adhikari is viral on social media, purporting to show him make several controversial statements. In the video, Adhikari starts off by expresses his satisfaction at winning the seats of Bhabanipur and Nandigram. Then, he goes on to say: “We will work only for Hindus, and exterminate the 27% Muslim population in West Bengal. In this way, our work will ensure that the BJP (Bharatiya Janata Party) stays in power for more than 100 years.”
X user (@Naira_Seth1) posted the viral clip, adding, in the caption, that this was a case of “majoritarian extremism” dressed as politics. (Archive)
Suvendu Adhikari openly bragged about defeating Mamata Banerjee in Nandigram, then reportedly declared that BJP will “only work for Hindus” and “eliminate the 27% Muslims of West Bengal” to stay in power for 100 years.
This is not governance. This is majoritarian extremism pic.twitter.com/MyIgERbyD2
— Naira Seth (@Naira_Seth1) May 7, 2026
X user (@Divyayadav98178) posted this video, which managed to rack up more than 8,000 views and over 50 shares. An archived version of the post, which has now been removed from the platform, can be accessed here.
X user (@nilakshirabhaa) had also posted the clip with the viral claim. It was later taken down.
Fact Check
It is clear from the video that it is AI generated. The man seen in the later part of the clip is not Suvendu Adhikari. See below:
Neither does the voice match Adhikari’s.
The microphones placed in front of the speaker display garbled and distorted text, which is a common visual indicator of artificially generated videos. See below:
On inspecting the video, we noticed several other inconsistencies with the accent, the lip sync, as well as the movements of the head, mouth, and hands.
We also ran relevant keyword searches to check if any news outlet had published a report on the controversial statements, but could find none.
We ran the video through Hive’s AI detection tool, which showed a strong possibility of it being artificially created, peaking at 98.8% around the 0:25 timestamp.
Thereafter, we broke down the video into keyframes. A reverse image search on one such keyframe led us to an excerpt of a press byte given by Suvendu Adhikari. It was uploaded by PTI, on May 6.
VIDEO | Kolkata, West Bengal: BJP’s Suvendu Adhikari says, “I urge everyone to remain calm, all TMC goons will be tackled through law.”
(Full video available on PTI Videos – https://t.co/n147TvrpG7)#Kolkata pic.twitter.com/XK6SAhLG8q
— Press Trust of India (@PTI_News) May 6, 2026
In this excerpt, Suvendu Adhikari promises that lawful action will be meted out to criminals. He makes no mention of Hindus or Muslims.
To sum up, the viral video, allegedly showing West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari promising to exterminate the Muslim population in West Bengal to ensure BJP’s stay in power, is AI-generated and not genuine.

title: Congress, TMC leaders share old video as post-poll violence in Bengal
body_text:
The results of the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections were declared on May 4. The Bharatiya Janata Party secured an absolute majority with 207 seats, while the ruling Trinamool Congress was reduced to 80. Following the results, reports of violence, arson, and vandalism targeting political offices emerged from several parts of the state.
Amid this, a video linked to the West Bengal Assembly election results has gone viral on social media. It shows a massive fire engulfing several shops as people scramble to evacuate goods from nearby establishments. A crowd can be seen gathered at the spot, while firefighters and a fire engine attempt to douse the blaze. The video also shows a Trinamool Congress flag hoisted atop one of the shops. Many users have shared the clip claiming it depicts post-poll violence and vandalism in West Bengal.
Congress leader Srinivas BV also shared the viral video, taking a swipe at the BJP’s landslide victory by claiming that “achhe din” had finally arrived in Bengal. (Archive link)
Sharing the video, Trinamool Congress MP Kirti Azad wrote that Amit Shah’s goons were involved in riots, arson, looting, and theft in West Bengal. The Godi media is busy praising the BJP’s fraudulent victory, he claimed. (Archive link)
A user named Mohammad Asif Abbas tweeted the viral video, writing that all the goons, criminals, terrorists, and rapists in West Bengal who were hiding in their holes out of fear of Mamata Banerjee had come out because their leader has formed the government. (Archive link)
पश्चिम बंगाल में अब तक जितने भी गुंडे, बदमाश, आतंकवादी और बलात्कारी ‘ममता बनर्जी’ के ख़ौफ़ से बिल में दुबक कर बैठे थे… वो सभी बिल से बाहर निकल आए हैं…
क्योंकि उन के सरबराह की सरकार बन गई है ! pic.twitter.com/Koz5LijXC4
— mohd Asif Abbas (@MOHDASIFABBAS3) May 4, 2026
Media outlet Brut India shared the viral video on its Instagram handle, linking it to the violence and vandalism that were reported from West Bengal. YouTuber Dhruv Rathee also used this Brut India report in his video. And this video was reshared by Trinamool MP Mahua Moitra.
Fact Check
We performed a reverse image search on Google with key frames from the viral video. We found the video uploaded by a Facebook user named SK Biky on April 25. Since the results of the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections were announced on May 4, the video cannot be related to post-poll violence.
Some users claimed this video was from a fire in Santoshpur on the suburbs of Kolkata. Alt News, however, could not verify this independently. However, it is conclusively proven that the video as been on the internet even before the second phase pf polling was held in West Bengal (on April 29).
Therefore, it cannot be a scene of post-poll violence.

title: Ijtema video from January falsely viral as illegal immigrants leaving Bengal post BJP win
body_text:
A 36-second-long video is getting viral on social media, with the claim that droves of illegal Bangladeshi immigrants are fleeing West Bengal after BJP won the assembly elections in the state.
Instagram user ‘theiconictrolls’ posted the viral video and garnered over 350,000 views.
X user ‘UttarndhraNow also posted the same video with a similar claim. This post from has 2,500 likes and close to 200,000 views. (Archive)
బెంగాల్ ను వదిలి ఎక్కడికి పోతున్నారు వీరంతా ?
మొన్నటి నుండే మొదలయిన ప్రయాణాలు. pic.twitter.com/puAzbr3pBY— UttarandhraNow (@UttarandhraNow) May 5, 2026
Another X user named ‘Yatnal Hindu Sene’ posted the same video with the caption, “All Bangladeshi Jihadisss must be kicked out soon, just watch how many jihadiiii P!gs in one area.”. This post has over 200,000 views. (Archive)
All Bangladeshi Jihadisss must be kicked out soon, just watch how many jihadiiii P!gs in one area. pic.twitter.com/3ynTH1ZN0d
— 🔱🚩YATNAL HINDU SENE 🚩🔱 (@yathnalabhimani) May 5, 2026
The same video was posted by other social media users echoing a similar claim, helping this video getting viral.
Alt News received multiple requests on its WhatsApp helpline to verify the video.
Fact Check
In the video itself, the ‘presenter’ says that the crowd is on its way to participate in an Ijtema.
We did a a simple reverse image search with some key frames from the viral video and found that a content creator called Dip Ghosh had uploaded the same video from his Instagram handle ‘youtuberdip‘ on January 5, 2026. The caption says: ‘Biswa Ijtema 2026 Insane crowd’.
At the 17-second mark, he says, “The Bishwa Ijtema is taking place in our area, where Muslims from all across the world have come to participate.”
Through a keyword search, we were able to verify the fact that a global Ijtema or Bishwa Ijtema, a religious congregation of Muslims from the Indian sub continent, was held in Hooghly district’s Dhanekhali assembly constituency area from January 2 to 5, 2026. The four-day gathering was occurring after a 34-year gap in the region.
Bengali daily Anandabazaar Patrika covered the event. The report, published on January 2, can be read here. Another report by Ei Samay can be read here. Both the stories mentioned the mammoth size of the gathering at the religious meet.
To sum up, a video related to a Muslim religious congregation from January is being falsely shared as illegal immigrants leaving Bengal after BJP’s win.

title: T20 World Cup revelry video from Prayagraj shared as celebration of BJP win in Bengal
body_text:
A 17-second video clip showing an enormous, visibly enthusiastic crowd waving Indian flags along with saffron flags, accompanied by a Hindutva-themed pop song in the background, is widely circulated on social media. It is claimed that the footage captures public celebrations in West Bengal, allegedly after the fall of Mamata Banerjee’s government.
NDTV senior executive editor Aditya Raj Kaul shared the video, claiming it showed celebrations in West Bengal by BJP supporters. (Archive)
West Bengal Celebrations by the BJP supporters. Jai Shri Ram chants all across! pic.twitter.com/ZYXERnQOGi
— Aditya Raj Kaul (@AdityaRajKaul) May 5, 2026
Journalist Abhijit Majumder did the same, predicting that Bengal “will be India’s biggest Hindutva laboratory.” (Archive)
West Bengal, now.
Mark my words: In a few years, it will be India’s biggest Hindutva laboratory. pic.twitter.com/8aVzZCcrSj— Abhijit Majumder (@abhijitmajumder) May 5, 2026
An X user, @chixxsays, shared the purported video clip, asserting that it shows Bengalis rejoicing after her defeat. (Archive)
Mamata was like Khamenei of India. No wonder people celebrated her defeat like this pic.twitter.com/2n1mkNEiH8
— ` (@chixxsays) May 5, 2026
The post garnered over 460,000 views.
Several other influential accounts shared the video, including The Skin Doctor, journalist Ashok Srivastav and Sheetal Chopra.
The video is also viral on Instagram. (Archive)
Fact Check
A reverse image search on a few keyframes led us to an Instagram post by India Today dated March 9, 2026. The post featured images of nationwide celebrations following India’s T20 World Cup victory the previous day. One of the photos is from Prayagraj. We found that the viral video shows the same scene as depicted in this photo.
Check the comparison below:
Taking hints from our initial findings, we conducted a keyword search that led us to a 4-minute video on YouTube, uploaded by a channel named ‘Mini Singh’. The video caption indicates that it showcases the celebration of India’s T20 World Cup victory in Loknath, Prayagraj, describing it as “the wildest celebrations in India.”
We noticed the same circular frame suspended in the sky that appears in the viral video. Additionally, we identified a political banner in the background that also matches the one seen in the video.
To corroborate further, we located the Baba Lokenath Dwar in Prayagraj, which is visible in the YouTube video, and the shop named Suman Ornaments which is visible in the viral video. This two are opposite each other.
Finally, we located a video uploaded on Instagram on March 9, where both the celebrating crowd and the ‘Suman Ornaments’ signage are visible. The caption says प्रयागराज में बना तगड़ा माहौल.
View this post on Instagram
This conclusively proves that the viral video has no connection to West Bengal or its electoral outcome. It is approximately two months old and captures celebrations in Prayagraj following India’s Men’s T20 World Cup victory. The claim that it depicts public reaction to political developments in West Bengal is false.

title: Video from Bangladesh viral as CAPF forces thrashing Muslim voters in Bengal
body_text:
A video, about 14 seconds long, is viral on social media, with the claim that it shows paramilitary personnel thrashing Muslim voters with sticks on voting day in West Bengal.
X user Khushi Ambedkar (@PatelKhush51313) posted the viral clip, writing that six Muslim women had reportedly been raped by the security forces. (Archive)
Allegations have surfaced claiming that security forces in West Bengal, reportedly under orders from Rajnath Singh, committed violence against Muslims, including reports of six Muslim women being raped. These claims remain serious and require impartial investigation. pic.twitter.com/BSKchRdGwn
— Khushi Ambedkar (@PatelKhush51313) May 3, 2026
Another X user, Sidd, (@sidd_sharma01) also posted the viral clip in the context of the West Bengal election. (Archive)
Why are these people being, hit
Can anyone indicate!!! pic.twitter.com/1KoxUGS58g
— 𝐒𝐢𝐝𝐝 (@sidd_sharma01) April 29, 2026
Several other users, like @Jai__Samvidhan, @santoshgazipur, @RishiAjnoti, @jeetusp and others also amplified the video on X. (Archives: 1, 2, 3, 4)
For screenshots, see below:
The video was also viral on Facebook, with similar claims.
It was also viral on Instagram. User @sagar_maurya8874 posted the clip. At the time of this article being written, the clip has garnered more than 60,000 views, and received more than 4,000 likes.
View this post on Instagram
Other users on Instagram also alleged that the clip showed scenes from the West Bengal election.
Fact Check
We noticed that the logo of “Law TV” was visible in the top-right corner. On looking it up, we found that the logo corresponds to a Bangladesh-based news channel.
Thereafter, to verify the authenticity of the claims surrounding the viral clip, we broke it down into keyframes.
A reverse image search on one such keyframe from the video led us to this Facebook video, uploaded by the Facebook page of “Law TV News”, on February 15.
সিভিলিয়ানের সাথে সেনাবাহিনীর আচরণ নির্বাচনের দিন ছিল মা’রমুখী ।
বহিরাগত সিভিলিয়ানের সাথে সেনাবাহিনীর আচরণ নির্বাচনের দিন ছিল মা’রমুখী ।
Posted by Law Tv News on Sunday 15 February 2026
The caption, which is in Bengali, states: “The military’s treatment of outsider civilians was brutal on election day.”
We checked further and made sure that the news outlet is based in Dhaka, Bangladesh.
To sum up, the video viral in the context of the West Bengal assembly elections, was originally uploaded in February, by a news agency based in Bangladesh. The claims that it shows CAPF forces attacking Muslim voters in West Bengal are misleading.

title: BJP, media outlets share Holi video as people celebrating BJP victory in Bengal
body_text:
A viral video claiming to show people in West Bengal celebrating the victory of the Bharatiya Janata Party in the Bengal assembly elections on May 4, 2026, is being widely shared on social media. The 27-second clip shows a large crowd chanting “Jai Sri Ram” on Howrah Bridge, giving the impression of post-election celebrations.
The video was also amplified by BJP’s Goa unit on Instagram, which shared a compilation suggesting that the gathering was in honour of the BJP’s electoral victory.
View this post on Instagram
This post received 7,600 views.
Right-wing X user Rishi Bagree also shared the video, with a caption implying that the celebrations on Howrah Bridge were due to the BJP coming to power. (Archive)
BJP win celebration at Howrah Bridge pic.twitter.com/fxCKyBG6KG
— Rishi Bagree (@rishibagree) May 4, 2026
His post garnered approximately 390,000 views.
BJP supporter Raushan Sinha, who runs the X handle @MrSinha, shared the same video, claiming that people chanting “Jai Sri Ram” on Howrah Bridge was something “unimaginable just a few years ago.” He described it as a “turning point in India’s electoral history and civilization.” (Archive)
Howrah Bridge echoing with chants of “Jai Shree Ram”, something unimaginable just a few years ago…
The hold of Mamata Banerjee is fading, and the streets are speaking. This is a turning point in India’s electoral history and our civilization 🇮🇳🕉️ pic.twitter.com/qLtGz50QYx
— Mr Sinha (@Mrsinha) May 4, 2026
This post accumulated around 25,500 views. It is worth noting that Sinha peddles misinformation and communal propaganda on a regular basis.
Sushant Sinha, the consulting editor for Times Now NavBharat, posted the video on May 4, suggesting that “No one had ever imagined that the brainwashing of Bengal’s Hindus during the Left’s 34-year rule could be undone by the Modi magic, allowing Bengal’s Hindus to unite like this.” (Archive)
लेफ्ट ने 34 साल के राज में बंगाल के हिंदुओं का जो ब्रेन वॉश किया था वो मोदी मैजिक में खत्म हो सकता है और बंगाल का हिंदू यूं एकजुट हो सकता है ये किसी ने सोचा नहीं था..
बंगाल में गूंजते ये जय श्री राम के नारे गजबे हैं.. 😃 pic.twitter.com/RTcZI0O8ft
— Sushant Sinha (@SushantBSinha) May 4, 2026
This post garnered approximately 100,000 views.
Multiple news outlets, including Pune Mirror, Times Now Navbharat, and News18 India, shared the purported video with similar claims.
Several social media users shared the video, claiming it showed people celebrating the BJP’s victory in the assembly elections.
Fact Check
A reverse image search of keyframes from the viral clip led us to an Instagram post dated March 4, 2026, exactly two months before the election results. The caption identifies the gathering as part of Holi celebrations on Howrah Bridge, with large crowds seen celebrating amid colours and chants of “Jai Sri Ram.”
It is the same video that has now gone viral.
The caption reads: “Jai Sri Ram! This time, Holi was celebrated with great enthusiasm on Howrah Bridge. Amidst colours, gulal, and excitement, the chant of ‘Jai Sri Ram’ echoed everywhere,” clearly indicating that the video is unrelated to any political event and predates the claim being made.
View this post on Instagram
Upon further investigation, we discovered several vlogs on YouTube that referred to a Rolls-Royce Holi celebration in Kolkata, which took place on March 1, 2026. One YouTube channel, Backbencher Street, published a vlog on that same day, capturing similar visuals and clearly audible chants.
At the 12:41 timestamp in the vlog, a crowd can be heard chanting ‘Jai Sri Ram.’ Additionally, at 2:52, the same vehicle visible in the very first frame of the viral video can be seen.
There are multiple other vlogs documenting the same Holi celebrations.
The Bagla family’s Rolls-Royce Holi is a unique, century-old tradition in Kolkata, where a vintage 1921 Rolls-Royce Silver Ghost, once owned by author Rudyard Kipling, is used as a chariot for the idols of Radha and Krishna.
